EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 110 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.5 MILES...WITH A WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. ONE HOUSE WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED...AND A FEW OTHER HOUSES AND BARNS HAD SOME DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED OFF.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE FROM ROUGHLY EXIT 56 ON I-81 TO EXIT 10 ON I-26. SEVERAL DAMAGED OR DESTROYED HOMES AND OTHER STRUCTURES. LARGE TREES DOWN THROUGHOUT THIS AREA.

EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 7.1 MILES...WITH A WIDTH OF 300 YARDS. MANY TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE PATH. SOME HOUSES HAD STRUCTURAL DAMAGE...AND ONE LARGE BARN SUFFERED HEAVY DAMAGE.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE NEAR GRAY. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES.

EF3 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS BETWEEN 140 AND 150 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 4.65 MILES...WITH A WIDTH OF 800 YARDS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG THE ENTIRE PATH. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE VARIED FROM MINOR TO EXTENSIVE...WITH THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE NOTED IN THE SPEEDWELL AREA NEAR THE END OF THE TRACK. TEN HOMES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED NEAR SPEEDWELL ALONG OLD HWY 63.
10 HOMES COMPLETELY DESTROYED IN SPEEDWELL ALONG OLD HWY 63. REPORTED BY EMERGENCY MANAGER.
